THIS WEEK IN LOCAL GOVERNMENT

Marathon County learned it was approved for FEMA individual assistance — but denied public assistance — after April's storms, while Kronenwetter and Weston boards moved forward on construction, development, and election-related decisions.

  • Marathon County was approved for FEMA individual assistance following April 2026 storms but denied public assistance due to insufficient documented damages; 24 county residents have already applied at disasterassistance.gov.
  • The Kronenwetter Village Board approved an amended bid for a six-bay equipment garage at $814,815, adding a second restroom, and approved placement of a ballot drop box for the August primary and November general elections.
  • The Village of Weston Public Works Committee awarded the Concord Avenue and Bayberry Street reconstruction contract to HS Suns for approximately $2,248,819.90, including a sidewalk alternate on Aspen.
  • The Weston Plan Commission unanimously approved a conditional use permit for a 1,090-square-foot detached accessory dwelling unit at 4804 Shore Avenue, with a prohibition on short-term rental use.
  • Weston's Plan Commission also forwarded a recommendation of approval to the village board for the Bull Junior View 23-lot subdivision final plat.
  • Kronenwetter's Plan Commission is set to hold public hearings Monday on a zoning change for Marathon County Highway Department property — from B3, SF and AR to M2 General Industrial — and a conditional use permit for a residential pond.

Coming up: Monday brings a full slate, including Weston's Board of Trustees at 6:00 p.m. and Kronenwetter's revised Plan Commission meeting at 6:00 p.m. covering the industrial rezoning request; Wausau's Plan Commission meets Tuesday at 5:00 p.m.

Full summaries on the Meeting Tracker →
